message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-051 (fix) in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What was done: bearer-token scrub regex covers base64 chars + / =.
Regression test(s): test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this fix. Let me start by examining the changed file and the regression test.

25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-thomasjones-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense; does not touch paths/email/reasoning, which are
34	# legitimate provenance — see README caveat for the public-release consideration).
35	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
36	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
37	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
38	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
39	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
40	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
41	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
42	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
43	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
44	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
45	]
46	
47	
48	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
49	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
50	        if pat.groups == 2:
51	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
52	        elif pat.groups == 1:
53	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
54	        else:
55	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
56	    return text
57	
58	
59	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
60	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
61	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
62	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
63	
64	
65	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
66	    try:
67	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
68	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
69	        if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl":
70	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
71	        else:
72	            data = raw
73	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
74	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
75	        else:
76	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
77	        return {
78	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
79	            "bytes": len(data),
80	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
81	        }
82	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
83	        return None
84

1	"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).
2	
3	These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
4	on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
5	a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
6	stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import importlib.util
12	from pathlib import Path
13	
14	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
15	
16	
17	def _load(rel: str, name: str):
18	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
19	    assert spec and spec.loader
20	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
21	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
22	    return mod
23	
24	
25	def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
26	    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
27	    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
28	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
29	    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
30	    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
31	    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
32	    # no recognizable token fragment survives
33	    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
34	        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"
35	
36	
37	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
38	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
39	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
40	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
41	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
42	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place
43

content.text: The fix and test are clear. Let me verify the test discriminates by checking it passes now and would fail with the old regex.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 -c "
import re
# old regex (pre-fix): no + / =
old = re.compile(r'(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}')
text = 'Authorization: [REDACTED]'
print('OLD result:', old.sub('[REDACTED]', text))
"
input.description: Check old regex would leak fragments

content.text: The old regex leaks. Note: the leading fragment `eyJhbGc` is only 7 chars before the `+`, which is below the `{20,}` threshold, so the OLD regex matches nothing at all here, leaving the entire token. The test asserts `[REDACTED] in scrubbed` and that fragments are absent — both fail with the old regex. Let me confirm the test passes now.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 -c "
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('cap', 'scripts/capture_transcripts.py')
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
text = 'Authorization: [REDACTED]'
s = mod._scrub(text)
print('NEW result:', s)
print('REDACTED in:', '[REDACTED]' in s)
print('any leak:', any(f in s for f in ('eyJhbGc','payload/signature','/signature','iOiJIUzI1NiJ9')))
"

content.content:
NEW result: Authorization: [REDACTED]
REDACTED in: True
any leak: False

content.text: Fix works, test discriminates. Let me confirm the test's `/signature` fragment check truly only passes due to the fix and not via another pattern. The other OAUTH/KEY patterns don't match "Authorization: Bearer", so the bearer regex is the sole redactor here. Verdict confirmed.

input.reasoning:
Fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py:41 extends the bearer-token scrub regex character class from [A-Za-z0-9._\-] to [A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}, so base64/base64url tokens containing + / = are matched and redacted in full rather than truncated at the first such char. This is idiomatic and scoped; no regression (the broader class only matches within a `bearer\s+` prefix, so legitimate prose is unaffected, and the {20,} length floor avoids false positives).

The regression test test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars (tests/test_scripts.py:25) is discriminating: it loads the real script via importlib, scrubs "[REDACTED]", and asserts [REDACTED] is present AND that fragments (eyJhbGc, payload/signature, /signature, iOiJIUzI1NiJ9) do not survive. I confirmed empirically that the pre-fix regex (no +/=) matches NOTHING on this input — because the leading fragment before '+' is only 7 chars, below {20,} — leaving the entire token verbatim, so both the [REDACTED]-present assertion and the no-leak assertions fail on revert. The current code produces "Authorization: [REDACTED]" with no leaked fragments. No other pattern (sk-ant, gh, AKIA, OAUTH/KEY env patterns) matches this Authorization-header input, so the bearer regex is the sole redactor under test. Both appropriate and complete.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. Fix at scripts/capture_transcripts.py:41 correctly extends the bearer-token regex to cover base64 chars (+ / =), and the regression test discriminates (fails on revert because the old regex matched nothing on the test input, leaving the token verbatim).
